title: Testing Time: The Constant Wear On Stairs

official_requirements.measurements_required: team-defined measurements of tread geometry, wear depth, edge rounding, slope, material proxy, and repair indicators

measurements.description: Integer label for each stair tread, bottom to top.
Total usable tread width measured by tape.
Front-to-back tread depth measured by tape.
Maximum depression at the center walking line from straightedge/feeler gauge.
Depression at left walking band, same cross-section.
Depression at right walking band, same cross-section.
Rounded or lost material at the front nosing edge.
Rounded or lost material at the back edge.
Tread surface tilt along likely upward direction from phone inclinometer.
Portable rebound/scratch class or Shore proxy, no coring.
Non-destructive density proxy from published material match or ultrasonic meter.
measurements.tool: published material match or ultrasonic proxy

measurements.description: 0-5 visual score for discontinuity, mortar line, color shift, or patch boundary.
Photograph IDs, location notes, and visible anomalies.

worked_example_assumptions.purpose: deterministic calibration example showing how a filled measurement sheet is inverted; values are not official observations
worked_example_assumptions.passages_per_user: one stair user creates one passage across each tread used
worked_example_assumptions.measurement_method: straightedge plus feeler gauge, phone inclinometer, caliper, scaled photographs, visual patch score, and non-destructive material proxy

historical_prior.daily_life_prior: religious/civic public interior, intermittent ceremonies plus regular service traffic
historical_prior.known_renovation_markers: patch boundaries, tool marks, color discontinuity, and abrupt adjacent-step wear jumps

usage_frequency.model: inverse Archard-style wear balance: passages = observed_depth / material_wear_rate

direction_preference.model: front/back edge rounding asymmetry plus along-tread slope sign
direction_preference.interpretation: front-edge rounding is stronger than back-edge rounding, consistent with uphill foot push-off dominance

simultaneous_use.model: cross-sectional wear-band shape: center-dominant versus side-band wear
simultaneous_use.interpretation: side bands are present but weaker than the central channel, suggesting mostly single-file traffic with occasional paired movement

historical_prior.daily_life_prior: religious/civic public interior, intermittent ceremonies plus regular service traffic
historical_prior.known_renovation_markers: patch boundaries, tool marks, color discontinuity, and abrupt adjacent-step wear jumps

consistency_check.overall_consistency: mostly consistent, with one likely repaired tread that should be excluded or down-weighted in final age inference
consistency_check.recommended_action: run the inverse model twice: once with all treads and once excluding high patch-boundary treads; compare the age/traffic interval overlap

age_reliability.model: deterministic uncertainty grid over material wear coefficient and plausible daily traffic
age_reliability.reliability_rating: medium: age is identifiable only jointly with material wear rate and daily traffic prior

renovation_detection.model: adjacent tread discontinuity + patch boundary + tool-mark scoring

repair_candidates.interpretation: probable replacement/patch: low wear relative to neighbors plus visible boundary/tool evidence
probable replacement/patch: low wear relative to neighbors plus visible boundary/tool evidence

material_source_guidance.material: compare non-destructive hardness/density proxies and observed wear rate against candidate quarry or timber reference samples
stone_source_workflow: photograph and map stone color, grain, bedding direction, and tool marks
measure rebound/scratch hardness and ultrasonic/density proxy without coring
compute implied wear coefficient from age and traffic priors
accept a quarry source only if hardness/density/geology and implied wear coefficient overlap the reference range
wood_source_workflow: identify species from visible grain and non-destructive imaging where permitted
compare surface hardness and wear direction to species-specific wear literature
use construction-age prior; do not infer tree age from tread wear alone

worked_example_material_proxy.consistency: compatible with a medium-soft building stone under moderate long-term public traffic

daily_use_pattern.short_burst_vs_long_duration: mixed ceremonial peaks plus regular low-intensity circulation
